grader_components.part: Sarah Palin is former governor and (2008) VP nominee
Palin is considering a primary challenge to Murkowski
Laura Ingraham is a Fox News host
Laura Ingraham is considering a primary challenge to Murkowski

grader_missing_subclaim: Fox News host Laura Ingraham is considering a primary challenge to Murkowski
grader_rewrite_suggestion: Former governor and 2008 vice presidential nominee Sarah Palin is seen as considering a primary challenge to Murkowski.
grader_proofs_verified: Sarah Palin, the GOP 2008 vice presidential nominee and former governor of Alaska.
The tweet was widely viewed as a thinly-veiled threat to challenge Murkowski in her 2022 reelection campaign to the Senate.
grader_why: The Palin components (her titles and her implied 2022 challenge to Murkowski) are provable from this source, though the shown sentence alone omits her titles. However, Laura Ingraham is never mentioned anywhere in the source — neither as a Fox News host nor as considering a challenge (the only Fox News host named is Mark Levin). Because a whole component is attributed to the source but absent from it, the author must fix this via citation or rewrite.
fable_reasoning: Conjunction of two named challengers. Palin considering a challenge is supported (tweet = 'thinly-veiled threat to challenge Murkowski'); Laura Ingraham is entirely absent from the source. One subject proven + one absent = partial (Finding-1).

grader_components.part: LIPA placed in the gold category (TEF)
achieving the 15th highest award in the country

grader_missing_subclaim: achieving the 15th highest award in the country
grader_rewrite_suggestion: Teaching Excellence Framework (TEF): LIPA received the top Gold rating.
grader_proofs_verified: Our teaching received the top Gold rating in the Government's Teaching Excellence Framework (TEF) Awards, which were announced in June 2017.
grader_why: The Gold-category component is directly proven by the shown sentence stating LIPA received the top Gold rating in the TEF. However, the ranking component — 'the 15th highest award in the country' — appears nowhere in the source text; the source describes Gold as 'the top' rating and never gives a numeric national ranking. Because this subclaim is not provable from the cited source at all, the author must add a citation or rewrite it down to what the source supports.
fable_reasoning: Core proven: LIPA placed in the TEF Gold category. The specific ranking '15th highest award in the country' is absent. Core proven + ranking detail unproven = partial (Finding-1).

grader_components.part: founded in 1968
co-founder named Aelred Kurtenbach
co-founder named Duane Sander
founders were professors of electrical engineering
at South Dakota State University
in Brookings, SD

grader_missing_subclaim: the second founder named "Duane Sander" (and the given name "Aelred" for Kurtenbach)
grader_rewrite_suggestion: Daktronics was founded in 1968 by Al Kurtenbach and a fellow electrical engineering professor at South Dakota State University in Brookings, SD.
grader_proofs_verified: The company was founded by Al Kurtenbach and a fellow electrical engineering professor at nearby South Dakota State in 1968.
One reason Daktronics has thrived in a town of about 22,000 has been its proximity to South Dakota State in Brookings.
grader_why: The source names only "Al Kurtenbach" and an unnamed "fellow electrical engineering professor" — it never states the given name "Aelred" nor the second founder's name "Duane Sander," so those components cannot be proven from this source at all (author's addition). Because at least one component is unprovable from the source, the author's problem dominates. The remaining components (1968, EE professors, South Dakota State, Brookings) are provable — Brookings appears elsewhere in the source though not in the shown sentence — so those proof sentences are listed for the tool.
fable_reasoning: Core proven: founded 1968 by Kurtenbach (Al = Aelred) and a fellow electrical-engineering professor at South Dakota State (warehouse in Brookings, SD). Only the second founder's name 'Duane Sander' is unnamed in the source. Core proven + one name unproven = partial (Finding-1).

grader_components.part: Ronnie O'Sullivan was the defending champion
he decided not to compete this year

grader_missing_subclaim: Ronnie O'Sullivan was the defending champion
grader_rewrite_suggestion: Ronnie O'Sullivan decided not to compete this year, stating he would not enter any tournaments for the time being including this year's Premier League.
grader_proofs_verified: He said today: "I have decided not to enter any tournaments for the time being including this year's Premier League and forthcoming WPBSA ranking events.
grader_why: The shown statement proves that O'Sullivan decided not to compete this year (including the Premier League), so that component is supported by the source. However, nothing in the source states or establishes that he was the defending champion — that fact is never mentioned in his statement or anywhere in the text. Since a component attributed alongside the citation is not provable from the source at all, the author must add a citation or rewrite.
fable_reasoning: The distinctive news component (O'Sullivan decided not to compete this year) is proven verbatim; the background status 'was the defending champion' is not stated in the source. Core assertion proven + background unproven = partial (Finding-1).

grader_components.part: stepped down in December
took up post as advisor to the Saudi Arabia national football team
the post was financially lucrative

grader_rewrite_suggestion: He stepped down in December, leaving to take up the post as advisor to the Saudi Arabia national football team.
grader_proofs_verified: Allen returned to The Hawthorns to act as scouting advisor from January to May 1977 - then as manager from June 1977 to December 1977 before leaving to act as advisor to the Saudi Arabian national team.
grader_why: The shown sentence proves that Allen left in December 1977 to become advisor to the Saudi Arabian national team, covering the timing and the role. However, the descriptor 'financially lucrative' appears nowhere in the source, which says nothing about the post's remuneration. That is an unsourced author addition, so the author must add a citation for it or remove it.
fable_reasoning: Core proven: stepped down in December to become advisor to the Saudi Arabia national team. The descriptor 'financially lucrative' is absent from the source. Core proven + descriptor unproven = partial; matches pipeline partial flag.

grader_components.part: The film (The Shining) was selected for preservation in the National Film Registry
by the Library of Congress
quoted criterion "culturally, historically, or aesthetically significant"

grader_missing_subclaim: the direct quotation "culturally, historically, or aesthetically significant" attributed to the selection — the source never uses that wording (it says films were chosen "because of their cultural, historic and artist importance", with no "aesthetically significant" phrasing to quote)
grader_rewrite_suggestion: In 2018, The Shining was selected for preservation in the United States National Film Registry by the Library of Congress for its cultural, historic and artistic importance.
grader_proofs_verified: The Library of Congress announced Wednesday, Dec. 12, 2018, that the films "Jurassic Park," ''Brokeback Mountain" and "My Fair Lady" are among the 25 movies tapped for preservation this year.
The national library chose a few more memorable titles such as "The Shining," ″Eve's Bayou" ″Hud" and "Broadcast News."
The library selected movies for preservation because of their cultural, historic and artist importance since the registry began in 1988.
grader_why: This is a mixed case, so the author's problem dominates. Most components are provable from the source but not among the shown sentences: the single shown sentence lists only Jurassic Park, Brokeback Mountain, and My Fair Lady — not The Shining — while the source elsewhere confirms The Shining's selection, the 2018 date, and the Library of Congress. However, the claim's verbatim quotation "culturally, historically, or aesthetically significant" is nowhere in this source, which instead paraphrases the criterion as "cultural, historic and artist importance"; a direct quote cannot be sourced from paraphrase, so the author must add a citation for that wording or rewrite it.
fable_reasoning: Core proven: in 2018 the Library of Congress selected The Shining for preservation (Dec 12 2018 announcement, The Shining named, chosen for cultural/historic/artistic importance). The quoted criterion 'culturally, historically, or aesthetically significant' is paraphrased not verbatim in the source. Core proven + quoted attribution not verbatim = partial (Finding-1).

grader_components.part: final match in England / last game on British soil
date: 1 February 1958
Edwards scored the opening goal
United defeated Arsenal 5–4

grader_proofs_verified: More than 63,000 fans packed into Arsenal's ground for the visit of Manchester United on Saturday, February 1, 1958.

Duncan Edwards opened the scoring with a fierce shot from the edge of the area, Bobby Charlton beat Jack Kelsey on the half-hour and Tommy Taylor gave United a seemingly unassailable three-goal lead at half time.
Vic Groves went close to an equaliser but the 'Busby Babes' held out for a famous 5-4 victory.
Sadly, it was to be their last game on British soil.
grader_why: Every component of the claim is provable from the cited source, but the single shown sentence proves only that Edwards scored the opening goal. The date (February 1, 1958), the 5–4 result, and its being the team's last game on British soil all exist elsewhere in the same source and were not displayed. This is a tool display problem, not an author problem, so the missing sentences should be fetched.
fable_reasoning: Every component is in the source: date (1 Feb 1958), Edwards scored the opening goal, United beat Arsenal 5-4, and it was his final match in England ('Sadly, it was to be their last game on British soil'). False unsupported -> supported (Finding-1).
